title: Recover an ext4 fstab mount after a misleading boot timeout
prompt: The archive mount did not come back after boot. `mountrecover` currently reports `MISSING_DEVICE`, but the evidence in `incident/` was captured after the delayed disk appeared: `blkid` resolves the fstab UUID, the ext4 filesystem is clean, and the later boot log rejects ownership options copied from a different filesystem. Diagnose and repair the focused classification defect. Current device identity must decide whether the UUID is present; an earlier boot timeout is historical evidence, not proof that the device is still absent. For a present device, compare the fstab filesystem type with `blkid`, honor the read-only filesystem check, and identify incompatible ext4 options. When the filesystem is clean and the options are the cause, report `BAD_OPTIONS`, write a safe corrected fstab entry that preserves the UUID, mountpoint, `nofail`, systemd timeout, dump, and pass fields while removing only incompatible ownership options, and validate the candidate through `mount --fake --no-mtab --all --fstab`. Never perform a real mount, update the live mount table, or run a write-mode filesystem repair. Keep the report's evidence summary and recovery-mode reversal instructions intact. Do not modify the protected tests or incident captures.
